brief.headline: Auction tape calm, liquidity expanding, but real yields at GFC extremes signal duration compression risk ahead.
brief.thesis: The macro complex is pricing a benign scenario: auction demand healthy (BTC 2.76, indirect 54%), net liquidity expanding $162B over 1mo (TGA spending), and funding plumbing healed (Eurodollar stress 25.85 = ABUNDANT). However, real yields at 2.06% (GFC-bottom territory) and 2s10s at +46bps (near inversion) suggest term-premium compression is already priced, not a forward signal. Primary dealers are NOT yet loading duration ahead of a Fed pivot—su

auction_tape.key_signal: 4-week bill BTC 2.76, indirect 54%, PD 40%, no tail stress—auctions clearing normally.

primary_dealer_positioning.key_signal: NYFed survey stale (last Apr 27, no new data); no recent duration-loading signal detected.

funding_plumbing.key_signal: Eurodollar stress 25.85 (ABUNDANT regime), 6 of 8 signals unavailable but 2 live signals benign.

net_liquidity.key_signal: Net liquidity expanded $162B over 1mo (TGA -$176B spending, RRP/WALCL neutral); risk-asset tailwind.

fed_path.key_signal: Fed-speak NLP data stale (12 speeches in 30d, 1 new classified, no hawk-dove score update).

tic_flows.key_signal: TIC composite stress 0, regime STRONG_FOREIGN_DEMAND; foreign demand healthy.

loudest_macro_anomaly.signal: 10Y real yield (TIPS) at 2.06% = GFC-bottom extreme; 2s10s at +46bps near inversion
loudest_macro_anomaly.interp: Real yields at 2009 lows despite Fed at 3.62% funds rate; term-premium compression extreme. Curve inversion risk rising if nominal yields fall below 4.10%.

brief.most_actionable: SHORT TLT (92.50 entry, 90.50 target, 94.50 stop) for 4-6% portfolio size. Real yields at GFC extremes + calm auction tape + no dealer duration loading = asymmetric short setup into June FOMC. If Fed stays hawkish or refunding supply is heavy, TLT re-tests lows. Expected return +2 to +4% over 4-8 we

setup_samples.headline: Real yields at GFC extremes; sell TLT rallies into June FOMC as term-premium compression unwinds.
setup_samples.target: 90.50 (2.06% real yield → 2.25% real yield = ~200bps nominal yield rise)
setup_samples.stop: 94.50 (breaks above = Fed pivot signal, cover short)
setup_samples.expected_pnl: +2 to +4% (200bps duration move on 20Y bond ≈ 2-3% price move)
setup_samples.dv01_note: TLT DV01 ~$1.50/bp; 200bp move = $300 per $1M notional; size 4-6% of portfolio for 2-4% expected return.

smoking_guns_sample.signal: 10Y real yield 2.06% = GFC-bottom extreme; 2s10s +46bps near inversion
Net liquidity expanded $162B/mo but TGA balance at $830B (near depletion); expansion decelerating
Indirect demand 54% (healthy), no tail stress; auctions clearing normally = no flight-to-safety bid

catalyst.consensus: Market expects Fed to hold rates steady at 3.50-3.75%, with dovish tilt in dot plot (1-2 cuts priced for 2H26).
catalyst.what_dealers_are_pricing: Real yields at GFC extremes (2.06%) suggest dealers have ALREADY priced in dovish pivot; no further duration upside.
catalyst.consensus_vs_dealer_position: DIVERGENT—consensus expects dovish tilt, but dealer positioning (real yields compressed) suggests dovish move is already priced. If Fed stays hawkish or refunding supply is heavy, duration will sell off.

analog.what_happened: Bernanke signaled QE taper; market had front-run dovish pivot; real yields spiked 100bps; TLT fell 10%.

setup_samples.invalidation: If 10Y nominal yield breaks below 4.10% (intraday close) and holds for 3+ days, Fed pivot is real; cover TLT short and reassess.

setup_samples.headline: 2s10s at +46bps near inversion; flatten curve via short 10Y / long 2Y into FOMC.
setup_samples.instrument: ZB futures (short) / ZN futures (long) OR TLT (short) / IEF (long) spread
setup_samples.expected_pnl: +2 to +3% (26bps curve flattening = ~50bps move on 10Y, ~24bps on 2Y = net ~1.5-2% spread move)
setup_samples.dv01_note: Curve trade: ZB DV01 ~$1.80/bp, ZN DV01 ~$0.75/bp; ratio hedge ~2.4:1 (short 2.4 ZB per 1 long ZN) for DV01-neutral flattener. TLT/IEF spread: TLT DV01 $1.50, I

smoking_guns_sample.signal: No recent duration-loading signal; dealers not front-running dovish pivot
Indirect demand 54%, no tail stress; no flight-to-safety bid under long end

catalyst.consensus: Market expects Fed to hold rates, with dovish tilt; 2Y expected to fall faster than 10Y (curve steepens).
catalyst.what_dealers_are_pricing: Real yields at GFC extremes suggest curve is already compressed; if Fed stays hawkish, 10Y will hold up better than 2Y (curve flattens further).
catalyst.consensus_vs_dealer_position: DIVERGENT—consensus expects steepening, but dealer positioning (real yields compressed, no duration loading) suggests flattening continues.

analog.what_happened: Curve inverted as flight-to-safety bid long end; dealers forced to unwind basis trades; 2s10s fell to -50bps.

setup_samples.invalidation: If 2s10s steepens above +70bps (Fed pivot signal confirmed), cover flattener and reassess.

setup_samples.headline: 5Y5Y breakeven at elevated levels; short TIP / long IEF as inflation expectations roll over.
setup_samples.expected_pnl: +1 to +2% (24bps breakeven compression = ~1.5% move on TIPS)
setup_samples.dv01_note: TIP/IEF spread: TIP DV01 ~$1.40 (real duration), IEF DV01 ~$0.65; ratio ~2.2:1 for DV01-neutral breakeven short.

smoking_guns_sample.signal: 10Y real yield 2.06% (GFC-bottom), nominal 4.45% → 5Y5Y breakeven ~2.39% (elevated vs 2% target)
Net liquidity expanding $162B/mo; no stagflation signal
Indirect demand 54%, no inflation-flight bid; auctions clearing normally

catalyst.consensus: Consensus expects CPI to cool (headline ~2.5%, core ~2.3%); Fed to hold rates with dovish tilt.
catalyst.what_dealers_are_pricing: Breakevens at 2.39% suggest dealers expect inflation to remain sticky; but real yields at GFC extremes suggest dealers have already priced in dovish pivot.
catalyst.consensus_vs_dealer_position: DIVERGENT—consensus expects inflation to cool and Fed to pivot dovish, but breakevens remain elevated (dealers hedging inflation tail risk).

analog.period: 2021-2022 inflation spike + FOMC pivot (Nov 2021 taper, Mar 2022 hike)
analog.what_happened: Breakevens spiked to 2.7% (May 2022); then fell to 2.0% (Dec 2022) as Fed hiked and inflation rolled over.

setup_samples.invalidation: If June CPI prints hot (headline >3.0%, core >2.5%), cover breakeven short and reassess inflation risk.

catalyst_samples.consensus: Routine bill auctions; expected to clear normally with healthy demand.
catalyst_samples.what_to_watch: BTC ratio and indirect demand; if BTC falls below 2.5 or indirect below 50%, dealer absorption stress emerging.

catalyst_samples.consensus: Routine bill auction; expected to clear normally.
catalyst_samples.what_to_watch: BTC and indirect demand; no stress signal expected.

catalyst_samples.consensus: Headline CPI expected ~2.5%, core ~2.3%; inflation cooling narrative intact.
